prerequisites: A FreshBooks account (sign up at freshbooks.com).
A FreshBooks developer/OAuth application created in the developer dashboard at my.freshbooks.com/#/developer, which generates the client_id (client ID) and client_secret.
A redirect_uri registered on that application. It must use HTTPS and cannot contain query-string parameters.
An OAuth authorization completed against the application to obtain client_refresh_token (and optionally oauth_access_token).
The account_id and business_uuid for the target business, retrievable from the /auth/api/v1/users/me identity endpoint after authenticating.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0 (Authorization Code)
authMethods.summary: FreshBooks uses the OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow. Register an application to get client_id and client_secret, then direct the user to https://auth.freshbooks.com/oauth/authorize with response_type=code, your client_id, and the registered redirect_uri. Exchange the returned code at the token endpoint (https://api.freshbooks.com/auth/oauth/token) with grant_type=authorization_code to receive a bearer access token (oauth_access_token) and a refresh token (client_refresh_token). Refresh tokens are one-time-use: each refresh (grant_type=refresh_token) returns a new access token and a new refresh token, invalidating the prior one. Map these to the connector secret fields client_id, client_secret, redirect_uri, client_refresh_token, and oauth_access_token.

setupSteps.title: Create a FreshBooks developer application
setupSteps.body: Sign in to FreshBooks and open the developer dashboard at my.freshbooks.com/#/developer. Create a new application, giving it a name and a redirect URI. The redirect URI must use HTTPS and must not contain query-string parameters. FreshBooks generates a client ID and client secret for the application; record these as client_id and client_secret, and use the registered URI as redirect_uri.

setupSteps.title: Authorize and obtain tokens
setupSteps.body: Send the user to https://auth.freshbooks.com/oauth/authorize/?response_type=code&redirect_uri=<redirect_uri>&client_id=<client_id>. After the user grants access, FreshBooks redirects to your redirect_uri with an authorization code. POST that code to https://api.freshbooks.com/auth/oauth/token with grant_type=authorization_code, client_id, client_secret, code, and redirect_uri to receive an access token (oauth_access_token) and a refresh token (client_refresh_token). Provide client_refresh_token to the connector so it can mint fresh access tokens; oauth_access_token and oauth_token_expiry_date are optional.

setupSteps.title: Find your account_id and business_uuid
setupSteps.body: Call the identity endpoint GET https://api.freshbooks.com/auth/api/v1/users/me with the bearer access token. The response lists your businesses under business_memberships, each carrying an account_id and a business_uuid. Copy the account_id and business_uuid for the business you want to sync into the connector config.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter the required fields: client_id, client_secret, redirect_uri, and client_refresh_token (all secret), plus account_id and business_uuid. Optionally supply oauth_access_token and oauth_token_expiry_date if you already hold a valid access token.
